evidence_citations.excerpt: I keep wondering if I'm still fit for this job or if I should just quit
the thought that this project might not impact my career path keeps me from starting
Every time I sit down to work on the project, I end up scrolling through emails instead
I keep thinking the timer is just a way to beat procrastination, but every time I hit start, my inbox feels like a pot that keeps boiling over

transcript.text: Every time I sit down to work on the project, I end up scrolling through emails instead. I can't seem to get a good night's sleep, and the bills are piling up. I keep wondering if I'm still fit for this job or if I should just quit.

You're really struggling to focus on your project, and it's like your mind is elsewhere, worrying about all these other things like emails, sleep, and bills. It sounds like the pressure of your job and financial responsibilities is taking a toll on your sleep and overall well-being. I can sense the anxiety and self-doubt in your words, especially when you wonder if you're still fit for your job. It's like you're questioning your whole career and feeling really uncertain about your future. Yeah, that's a lot to carry around. What's the first thing that comes to mind when you think about what's holding you back from making progress on your project?

I think the main thing is that I keep scrolling through emails instead of focusing on the project, and that keeps me from moving forward. The lack of sleep and mounting bills make it hard to stay motivated, and I wonder if I'm still fit for this role. It feels like I'm stuck between the project and the financial pressure.

You're feeling really stuck right now, like you're being pulled in two different directions by the project and the financial pressure. It's like you're trying to juggle too many things at once, and it's hard to know where to start. The fact that you're scrolling through emails instead of focusing on the project suggests that you might be using that as a way to avoid the stress and uncertainty of the project itself. Yeah, it can be really tough to stay motivated when you're feeling overwhelmed like this. What's one thing that you think would help you feel more in control of your finances or your project, even if it's just a small step?

Maybe the first thing I can do is set a 15‑minute timer and just start on the project, like letting a slow‑braised dish sit for a few minutes before I add the spices. But every time I do that, I end up scrolling through emails instead, and the bills keep piling up. I’m not sure if this project even matters in the long run, which just adds to the anxiety about my career. I also can’t seem to fall asleep at night, so it’s a vicious cycle.

You're feeling really trapped in this cycle of procrastination and anxiety, and it's like you're waiting for the perfect moment to start, but it never comes. The fact that you're considering setting a 15-minute timer to start on your project shows that you're aware of the need to take small steps, but the fear of not being in control of your finances and career is holding you back. It's like you're trying to find a way to break free from this vicious cycle, but it's hard to know where to start. Yeah, it can be really tough to stay motivated when you're feeling overwhelmed like this.

I can see how a 15‑minute timer might help, but every time I try, my inbox pulls me back. The bills keep piling up and I can't seem to get any rest. I keep wondering if this whole project is worth it, given the uncertainty about my career and the financial strain.

You're feeling really stuck right now, like you're being pulled in two different directions by the project and the financial pressure. It's like you're trying to juggle too many things at once, and it's hard to know where to start. The fact that you're considering using a 15-minute timer to start on your project shows that you're aware of the need to take small steps, but the fear of not being in control of your finances and career is holding you back. Yeah, it can be really tough to stay motivated when you're feeling overwhelmed like this. It sounds like the uncertainty about your career and the financial strain is making it hard for you to see the value in the project right now.

I keep thinking the timer is just a way to beat procrastination, but every time I hit start, my inbox feels like a pot that keeps boiling over. The bills are piling like onions in a stew—no matter how many layers I add, the flavor still tastes off. I can't fall asleep, so the night feels endless. Maybe I need to slow down and focus on one thing at a time, but the uncertainty about my career and the financial strain just keeps simmering.

You're feeling like you're drowning in a sea of tasks and responsibilities, and it's hard to know where to start. The image of the inbox as a pot that keeps boiling over and the bills piling up like onions in a stew is a powerful one - it sounds like you're feeling like you're trying to cook a meal, but the ingredients just keep multiplying. Yeah, it can be really tough to stay motivated when you're feeling overwhelmed like this. Maybe taking a step back and focusing on one thing at a time could help, like taking a few deep breaths before you start cooking.

Maybe I should just clear the inbox first, but that feels like chasing a moving target. The project still feels vague, and my sleep hasn't improved at all. I don't know if putting it on hold will help me decide what to do with my career or my finances.

You're feeling really stuck right now, like you're trying to tackle too many things at once and it's hard to know where to start. The pressure of your inbox and the vagueness of your project are weighing on you, and the financial strain and career uncertainty are making it even tougher. It's like you're trying to find a way to clear the deck, but everything feels like a moving target. Maybe taking a step back to focus on one thing at a time could help, even if it's just a small step like setting that 15-minute timer again.

Yeah, I set the timer again, but the inbox keeps pulling me back. The bills pile up and I keep thinking about whether this project matters if my career path is still unclear. I can’t seem to get any rest either, so it’s a loop.

You've tried setting the timer again, but it's like your inbox has a strong pull on you, and the bills and career uncertainty are always lurking in the background. It's a loop that's hard to break, especially when you're not getting the rest you need. It sounds like the uncertainty about your career path is making it tough for you to see the value in the project right now. Maybe we can explore what's driving that uncertainty together.

Right, the loop feels real. The bills stack like onions in a stew, and I don't know which layer to tackle first. If I could break the project into bite‑sized chunks, maybe the fog over my career would clear, but I still can't get that pot of sleep to come to a boil.

The image of the bills stacking like onions in a stew is really powerful - it sounds like you're feeling like you're trying to cook a meal, but the ingredients just keep multiplying. Yeah, it can be really tough to stay motivated when you're feeling overwhelmed like this. Maybe taking a step back to focus on one thing at a time could help, even if it's just a small step like breaking down the project into bite-sized chunks. What do you think is the smallest, most manageable chunk you could tackle first?

I think the smallest chunk is getting the database schema right before I tackle the API layer, like seasoning before the main dish. Even so, the thought that this project might not impact my career path keeps me from starting. I also keep losing sleep over the bills piling up.

You're trying to tackle this project, but the uncertainty about your career path is making it hard to start, and the bills piling up are keeping you awake at night. Getting the database schema right before tackling the API layer feels like a manageable first step, but it's tough to prioritize when you're feeling overwhelmed. The thought of this project not impacting your career path is also weighing on you, making it even harder to begin. Yeah, it's like you're trying to season the dish before you even start cooking, but the ingredients just keep multiplying.

I keep scrolling through my inbox again even after setting the timer, and the bills keep piling. I still can't fall asleep, and if this project doesn't change my career direction, I'm not sure why I'm stuck doing it.

You're back to scrolling through your inbox, and the bills keep piling up, making it hard to fall asleep. It's like you're trying to find a way out of this loop, but everything feels overwhelming. The project feels uncertain, and you're questioning its impact on your career. It sounds like you're feeling really stuck, and the thought of not making progress is weighing on you. Maybe we can break down what's feeling most overwhelming right now, and see if there's a way to tackle one thing at a time.
